basics.name: Housam Ziad
basics.label: Peace Commissioner | Lecturer | Software Engineer
basics.summary: I'm a Software Engineer by day, a lecturer by night, and an electronics hobbyist in between – basically, I'm a 24/7 nerd. My journey into the tech world started back in '98 when I was just a wide-eyed Web Designer, but let's be honest, who wasn't in '98? Fast forward to 2008, and I made the genius decision to dive headfirst into Web Development. It was like going from playing with LEGO to building the actual skyscraper! 🎨 💻 ✨  Along the way, I soaked up more knowledge than a sponge at a TED talk. I'm talking Business Development, Marketing, Management – you name it! I even dabbled in Entrepreneurship, but don't ask me about the failed lemonade stand. Oh, and I've been rubbing digital elbows with international organisations, no big deal.  Now, my true calling? Making stuff look good! I'm passionate about crafting designs that are so drop-dead gorgeous they make the Mona Lisa look like a doodle on a napkin. Whether it's for print or the web, I'm on a quest for happyness, one pixel at a time.

work.summary: A Peace Commissioner is an honorary position that carries specific powers and duties, including taking statutory declarations, witnessing signatures on documents required by various authorities, and signing certificates and orders under various Acts. Additionally, according to the Courts of Justice Act 1924, Peace Commissioners have the authority to issue summons and warrants.

Empowering aspiring web developers as a lecturer for the Professional Diploma in Software Development at UCD Professional Academy. This program equips students with in-demand languages like HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python, and SQL to become full-stack developers. I create a dynamic learning environment, fostering expertise in web development fundamentals and practical application through real-world project development.

- Led and supported budding designers through the exciting realms of Game UI Design, Game Design, and Architecture, actively shaping their journeys in these dynamic fields. - Crafted comprehensive curricula for Game UI Design and Game Design & Architecture, ensuring students received a balanced blend of theory and practical know-how. - Designed engaging lessons that explored the psychology of UI Design, practical methodologies for creating functional UI, and strategies for delivering positive user experiences. - Oversaw day-to-day class activities, from interactive lectures to hands-on workshops, fostering an environment where students could thrive and experiment with their newfound skills. - Assigned projects and assignments that mirrored real-world challenges, providing students with invaluable experience and preparing them for the demands of the industry.

As Ireland's representative on the Oracle European Works Council (EWC), I bridge local perspectives with broader European discussions, contributing to company-wide initiatives and policy dialogue. In this role, I collaborate with representatives from various countries to ensure that the needs, concerns, and insights from our Irish teams are acknowledged in decisions that impact our European workforce. I am committed to fostering cross-cultural understanding, advocating for fair practices, and supporting Oracle's commitment to transparency and employee engagement across Europe.

As a member of the Employee Consultation Forum (ECF) in Ireland, I work closely with my colleagues to ensure their perspectives are represented within our local operations. I facilitate open communication between employees and management, addressing workplace issues, and supporting initiatives that enhance our working environment. My role focuses on advocating for employees' interests within Ireland, contributing to a collaborative culture that values transparency and inclusivity at all levels.

Craft exceptional user experiences (UX) for complex enterprise software at Oracle. Lead the design and development of the UI for the Private Cloud Appliance. Prioritise usability, efficiency, and user adoption to drive business value. Extensive experience in JavaScript, TypeScript, Oracle JET, and React. Passionate about building intuitive interfaces that empower users. Working with 300 engineers in different teams in an Agile development environment to create a state-of-the-art user experience that meets business goals.

- Instructed and guided students enrolled in the MSc and Diploma programs for Software Engineering & Database Technologies. - Served as a valuable member of the IT Online Programme team at NUI Galway. - Delivered courses on various subjects, including Software Engineering, Object-oriented Programming (Java), Fundamentals of Database, Artificial Intelligence, and Thesis Fundamentals.

work.company: Insight Research Ireland Centre for Data Analytics
work.summary: I worked on filling the gap between research and industry by converting research into real-world software, using my mixed business and technical skills. My personal research was to build an NLP platform, Teanga [1], that uses Linked Data to achieve interoperability among NLP services, a new approach to utilising UX in NLP. Teanga was well-received by domain experts and during LREC 2018 in Miyazaki, Japan. While working at the Insight Centre, I completed a Master’s degree with First-Class Honours in 2017.  Libraries: Angular, AngularJS, SASS, D3, Bootstrap, Python Flask, Jinja, Handlebars.  [1] http://www.teanga.io

I founded emind Creative Solutions to fulfil clients' web development and applications needs. My vision was to apply user-oriented design and use modern web technologies, including mobile-first design processes, to help clients achieve more in the era of hand-held devices while simultaneously serving users from laptops and desktop computers. For six years, emind worked with big clients like Nissan, Garmin, Honda, Infiniti, Lipton, Pepsi co, McVities, and others.
Leading the development team through the company's project. My tasks included collecting requirements from stockholders and project planning/management.
Teaching all-level classes of Programming, Adobe Suite, and Microsoft Office.

As an Electronics Assembly Operative at Beacon Electronic Industries, I assembled and tested electronic protection and monitoring circuits, including through-hole and SMD work. I carried out functional testing with standard bench equipment, diagnosed and repaired returned units, and supported engineers with prototype builds and early R&D tasks. The role combined hands-on production with problem-solving, quality awareness, and continuous improvement across the assembly process.

publications.name: Teanga: A Linked Data based platform for Natural Language Processing
publications.summary: In this paper, we describe Teanga, a linked data based platform for natural language processing (NLP). Teanga enables the use of many NLP services from a single interface, whether the need was to use a single service or multiple services in a pipeline. Teanga focuses on the problem of NLP services interoperability by using linked data to define the types of services input and output. Teanga’s strengths include being easy to install and run, easy to use, able to run multiple NLP tasks from one interface and helping users to build a pipeline of tasks through a graphical user interface.

publications.name: Mixedemotions: An open-source toolbox for multimodal emotion analysis
publications.summary: Recently, there is an increasing tendency to embed functionalities for recognizing emotions from user-generated media content in automated systems such as call-centre operations, recommendations, and assistive technologies, providing richer and more informative user and content profiles. However, to date, adding these functionalities was a tedious, costly, and time-consuming effort, requiring identification and integration of diverse tools with diverse interfaces as required by the use case at hand. The MixedEmotions Toolbox leverages the need for such functionalities by providing tools for text, audio, video, and linked data processing within an easily integrable plug-and-play platform. These functionalities include: 1) for text processing: emotion and sentiment recognition; 2) for audio processing: emotion, age, and gender recognition; 3) for video processing: face detection and tracking, emotion recognition, facial …

publications.name: OTTO-Ontology Translation System
publications.summary: To enable knowledge access across languages, ontologies that are often represented only in English, need to be translated into different languages. For this reason, we present OTTO, an OnTology TranslatiOn System, which enhances ontologies with multilingual information. Rather a different task than the classic document translation, ontology label translation faces highly specific vocabulary and lack contextual information. Therefore, OTTO takes advantage of the semantic information of the ontology to improve the translation of labels.

publications.name: Who are the american vegans related to brad pitt?: Exploring related entities
publications.summary: In this demo, we present Entity Relatedness Graph (EnRG), a focused related entities explorer, which provides the users with a dynamic set of filters and facets. It gives a ranked lists of related entities to a given entity, and clusters them using the different filters. For instance, using EnRG, one can easily find the American vegans related to Brad Pitt or Irish universities related to Semantic Web. Moreover, EnRG helps a user in discovering the provenance for implicit relations between two entities. EnRG uses distributional semantics to obtain the relatedness scores between two entities.

projects.name: Prêt-à-LLOD
projects.summary: In this project we aim to increase the uptake of language technologies by exploiting the combination of linked data and language technologies, that is Linguistic Linked Open Data (LLOD), to create ready-to-use multilingual data. Prêt-à-LLOD aims to achieve this by creating a new methodology for building data value chains applicable to a wide-range of sectors and applications and based around language resources and language technologies that can be integrated by means of semantic technologies, in particular the usage of Linguistic Linked Open Data (LLOD).

projects.name: Teanga, A Linked Data based Natural Language Processing Platform
projects.summary: Teanga is a linked data based platform for natural language processing (NLP).  Teanga enables the use of many NLP services from a single interface, whether the need was to use a single service or multiple services in a pipeline. Teanga focuses on the problem of NLP service interoperability by using linked data to define the types of service input and output. Teanga's strengths include being easy to install and run, easy to use, able to run multiple NLP tasks from one interface and helping users to build a pipeline of tasks through a graphical user interface.

projects.name: MixedEmotions
projects.summary: The MixedEmotions platform is a Big Data Toolbox for multilingual and multimodal emotion extraction and analysis. It can extract emotions from a text, audio and video. However, it also has many other capabilities, such as sentiment analysis, social network analysis and knowledge graphs visualisation among others.

projects.name: Smartie
projects.summary: Smartie is a tool built for the Irish Times journalists to get detailed insights into Irish Times archives about topics.

projects.name: Kenny's Advanced Browse
projects.summary: A project in cooperation with the oldest Book Shop in Galway, Kenny's. ‘Advanced Browse’ uses a combination of user reviews on Goodreads.com and the description of a book to create visual maps of book categories that you can expand and explore. Clicking on any ‘node’ on any branch of the tree will bring you to a list of books in that area, categorised by year of publication.
